sentence.text: You can hike all day in the Blue Ridge Mountains.
The air when you are walking on the trail is fresh and clear.
It smells like green trees and wild flowers.
The path is dark green and little light reaches there.
It is so thick with trees you cannot see the sky.
From far away, the early morning air looks like smoke.
It makes the color of the mountains a deep blue, like fog.
When the sun rises higher, some of the water in the air will burn off.
Then the mountains will slowly turn dark green.
CR
The mountain water is icy cold and sparkles in the sun.
You could drink it all day - it's that good!
The rough landscape takes your breath away with its scale and color.
Hikers should stick to the trails for a safe passage through the miles of wild, tough land.
You have to go there to smell and taste the huge backdrop that the mountains give to you.
The size of them is hard to take in from maps and books.
CR
CR
All too soon it will be time to leave.
It will be hard to go back to the world of cars, roads, stores and crowds of people.
But you will remember your time in the Blue Ridge Mountains.
CR
CR
Over the years there have been many songs made up about them, often performed by well-known artists.
